steps.title: Calculate the fixed and variable costs
steps.content:
The first 25 people cost: $$25 \times 21 = 525 \text{ dollars}$$
The remaining $(n - 25)$ people cost \$14 each.
Combining both parts:
$$f(n) = 525 + 14(n - 25)$$

steps.title: Expand and simplify the formula
steps.content:
Distribute the 14:
$$f(n) = 525 + 14n - 350$$
Combine constants:
$$f(n) = 14n + 175$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
